title: ANX-Bench Anchor Response Row Schema
description: Canonical non-scored export contract for one respondent-domain-severity anchoring-vignette rating. Anchor response rows support response-style calibration, monotonicity diagnostics, and sensitivity analyses, but they are not scored ANX item rows and cannot be exported under schema/wave_response.schema.json.

wave_id.description: Wave identifier for the Wave 8 full-domain bridge anchor export.

benchmark_version.description: Benchmark release version governing the Wave 8 anchor vignette set.

respondent_id_hash.description: SHA-256 or stronger salted, study-scoped pseudonymous respondent identifier. Direct identifiers are prohibited.

anchor_id.description: Stable Wave 8 anchoring-vignette identifier. This is not an ANX item_id.

domain.description: Wave 8 domain targeted by the anchor vignette.

intended_severity.description: Preregistered severity band for the anchor vignette.

raw_response.description: Observed 1 to 5 anxiety rating for the anchor vignette, or null when no answer was captured. Anchor responses are non-scored calibration data.

missingness_code.description: Missingness provenance for the anchor row. observed requires a non-null raw_response; every other value requires raw_response to be null.

response_timestamp.description: UTC timestamp at which the anchor response was submitted or finalized by the fielding system.

survey_weight.description: Final respondent-level analysis weight applicable to this non-scored anchor row. Use 1.0 until final weights are constructed.

domain_order_violation.description: Domain-level monotonicity descriptor repeated on all three anchor rows for the respondent-domain. True means at least one observed pair contradicts low <= moderate <= high; null means fewer than two ratings are observed.

domain_response_style_stratum.description: Wave 8 domain-specific response-style stratum derived from the low, moderate, and high anchor ratings.

overall_response_style_stratum.description: Respondent-level all-domain anchor response-style stratum repeated on each of the respondent's 21 anchor rows.
